solution: 
```class Solution:
    def binaryGap(self, n: int) -> int:
        max_gap = 0
        prev_one = -1
        while n:
            if n & 1:
                if prev_one != -1:
                    max_gap = max(max_gap, n - prev_one - 1)
                prev_one = n
            n >>= 1
        return max_gap
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates over the binary representation of the input number from right to left, keeping track of the position of the last '1' encountered. When a '1' is found, it calculates the gap between the current '1' and the previous one, updating the maximum gap if necessary. This approach has a high efficiency due to its constant space complexity and logarithmic time complexity.
